termination_clause: Section 10.1: Term - initial term three years from Launch Date, automatically renews for successive one-year terms unless either party terminates at least six months prior to expiration. Section 10.2: Breach - Either party may terminate if the other is in material default or breach and fails to cure within 60 days after written notice. Section 10.3: Failure of Negotiations After Initial Term - If parties cannot renegotiate revenue sharing terms at end of Initial Term, agreement terminates six months from conclusive determination.
confidentiality_clause: Section 11.1: 'All communications and information disclosed by one party to the other party under this Agreement shall be subject to the terms and conditions of the CNDA.' Section 1.4 defines CNDA as the Confidential and Non-Disclosure Agreement executed contemporaneously. Section 6.4 further specifies that Dassault Systemes Customer Databases and customer input/output data are confidential information of Dassault Systemes and will be treated as per the CNDA.

EVEN IF THAT PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH LOSS OR DAMAGE. Additionally, Sections 9.1-9.3 outline indemnification obligations for breaches and intellectual property infringement.
summary: This Co-Branding Agreement between Dassault Systemes and PlanetCAD establishes a partnership to develop and offer a co-branded translation and healing web service (Co-Branded Service) based on PlanetCAD's 3Dshare.com and Dassault's CBD Software. PlanetCAD initially hosts the service and provides second-level support, while Dassault provides first-level support and customer access via its websites. Revenue is shared based on hosting responsibility (redacted percentages). Key obligations include PlanetCAD's hosting and maintenance, Dassault's marketing and support, and mutual cooperation on development. Notable risks: strict limitations on liability (no consequential damages, cap equal to 12 months' revenue share), termination rights for uncured material breach, and post-termination restrictions on PlanetCAD marketing to Dassault customers. Confidentiality is governed by a separate CNDA. Intellectual property ownership remains with each party, with limited licenses.

termination_clause: Section 1.3 provides that a six (6) months notice must be given by either party of their intention to terminate relations due to any reason other than breach of this agreement. Section 7.3 states that each party shall have the right to summarily terminate this Agreement: (a) on the committing of a material breach (with 21-day cure period if rectifiable); (b) on the commencement of winding up or bankruptcy, appointment of a receiver, or ceasing business for 30 consecutive days; (c) on either party being substantially prevented from performing its obligations; (d) on assignment without prior written consent; (e) if control of either party passes to persons the other party regards as unsuitable; (f) if either party's voting stock is transferred resulting in change of effective control. Termination takes effect immediately upon written notice.

Section 6.2 requires the Customer to maintain product liability insurance naming the Manufacturer as Additional Insured. Section 3.5(a) states that the Manufacturer indemnifies the Customer for any Intellectual Property claims. Section 14.7 provides that neither party is liable for a breach caused by force majeure events such as hostilities, government acts, natural disasters, industrial action, or any cause beyond reasonable control.
summary: This ODM Supply Agreement appoints AGAPE ATP INTERNATIONAL HOLDING LIMITED as the exclusive global agent for the promotion, sales, marketing, distribution, and administration of specified products manufactured by ORGANIC PREPARATIONS INC. The Manufacturer must comply with laws, maintain manufacturing and packaging standards, provide documentation, and not sell the listed products to others without Customer consent. The Customer must comply with laws, use best efforts for market penetration, pay 50% of order cost upfront and 50% before shipment, maintain product liability insurance, and inspect products within 7 days with the right to reject defective items. The agreement has a 10-year term with automatic renewal unless 6 months' notice is given, and can be terminated summarily for material breach, insolvency, change of control, or other specified events. The Manufacturer indemnifies the Customer against IP claims, and force majeure excuses liability. Minimum annual purchase requirements are set in Schedule B, and the Customer has a first right of refusal to purchase the intellectual property.

summary: The agreement is between ARCA U.S. TREASURY FUND and ARCA CAPITAL MANAGEMENT, LLC (the Blockchain Administrator) for the development of ERC-1404 compatible digital securities and administration of the underlying smart contracts. The Blockchain Administrator must perform or oversee development, maintenance, and administrative services, maintain records, and conduct relations with service providers. The Fund pays an annual fee of 0.20% of average net assets and bears most operating expenses. Notable risks include a broad limitation of liability and indemnification in favor of the Blockchain Administrator, which protects it except for criminal conduct, willful misfeasance, bad faith, gross negligence, or reckless disregard of duties. The agreement may be terminated by either party upon 60 days' notice without penalty.

summary: This Addendum I to the Endorsement Agreement dated October 30, 2017, adds Gridiron CBD H2O Probiotic Water to the list of Licensed Products. The parties are NFLA-NC, NFLA, and the Company. Key obligations: The Company must pay a donation of $0.05 per unit sold of Licensed Products to NFLA-NC on a quarterly basis, with the first payment following the first full quarter. NFLA-NC then donates 15% of those proceeds to NFLA. The Company must provide quarterly sales reports upon request. No specific penalties or risks are stated in this addendum, but failure to report or pay could lead to breach of the underlying agreement. The addendum does not include termination, confidentiality, or liability provisions, which would likely be found in the original Endorsement Agreement.

termination_clause: Either Party may terminate by providing a Termination Notice at least 30 days prior to the end of the Initial Term or any Renewal Term (Section 11.1). Either Party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches a material term and fails to cure within 30 days for payment breaches or 45 days for other breaches, with an additional 180 days allowed if the breach is not reasonably curable within the cure period and diligent cure is commenced (Section 11.2).

Each Party also indemnifies the other against third-party claims arising from breaches of representations, negligence, IP infringement, or unauthorized use of marks (Sections 15.4, 15.5).
summary: This Co-Branding Agreement between VerticalNet and Neoforma establishes a partnership to co-brand online career and training centers and to exchange product listings for medical and laboratory equipment. VerticalNet agrees to provide its medical product listings exclusively to Neoforma and develop co-branded sites, while Neoforma provides laboratory product listings exclusively to VerticalNet and promotes the co-branded centers. Both parties grant licenses to use each other's content and marks, share revenue from commissions and advertising, and undertake co-marketing efforts. Notable risks include a $1,000,000 cap on liability (except for confidentiality breaches and indemnification obligations), mandatory binding arbitration in Pennsylvania, and specific cure periods for termination upon material breach. The agreement includes detailed confidentiality provisions with three-year post-termination survival and mutual indemnification for IP infringement and other claims.

termination_clause: Section 11.2: This Agreement may be terminated as follows: (a) immediately upon mutual agreement; (b) immediately by a non-breaching party if the other party materially breaches and fails to cure within 90 days after notice; (c) immediately upon bankruptcy, insolvency, or assignment for benefit of creditors by a party; (d) by any party if SMBC Unit-Holders cease to hold any Partnership Interests as a result of sale or transfer, effective six months after notice; (e) immediately by Moelis Holdings if Closing has not occurred by March 1, 2012. Section 11.3: Upon termination, no party shall have any liability except for prior breaches or payment obligations, and certain sections survive.

Confidential Information does not include information already in possession, obtained from a third party without confidentiality obligation, or independently developed. summary: The Strategic Alliance Agreement establishes a non-exclusive alliance among Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corporation, SMBC Nikko Securities Inc., and Moelis & Company Holdings LP to cooperate on investment banking businesses (M&A, restructuring, capital markets, risk advisory, and merchant banking) involving Japanese companies in specified regions. Key obligations include sharing fees 50/50 on joint assignments, non-solicitation of employees and clients, maintaining confidentiality, a secondment program, and joint marketing. Moelis Holdings agrees not to open a Japan office. Notable risks include termination for material breach (with 90-day cure), immediate termination upon bankruptcy, and extensive indemnification obligations (Article XIII) for securities registration, with liability limited to proceeds received. Non-solicitation covenants survive termination for 12 months.

summary: This Addendum amends the existing Zebra PartnerConnect Distributor Agreement to add Xplore Products to the product portfolio, allowing Distributor to purchase and resell them. Key obligations include: Distributor places purchase orders via email, subject to Xplore's acceptance; orders are non-changeable after acceptance except within specified business days; Distributor must use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain 30 days of inventory; shipping terms are DDP (Delivery Duty Paid), with title and risk passing on delivery. Notable risks include: Xplore may allocate inventory at its discretion if production is off schedule; purchase orders for large volumes may be subject to alternative terms; regulatory approvals may delay sales; the warranty for Xplore Products differs from Zebra's standard. Termination follows the Distribution Agreement's provisions. No separate confidentiality or liability clauses appear in this Addendum; those are governed by the underlying Distribution Agreement.

summary: This non-exclusive distributor agreement appoints SUNWAI Technology as a distributor of iMine Corporation's cryptocurrency mining rigs in Taiwan. The Distributor must use best efforts to promote sales, maintain adequate inventory, provide sales forecasts and reports, and not sell competitive products. iMine must supply products, set prices, and accept orders subject to terms. Notable risks: the Distributor bears all shipping and import costs, orders are generally non-cancelable, and late payments incur 1.5% monthly interest. iMine provides no warranty (disclaims all implied warranties) and limits its liability to amounts received, excluding consequential damages. The Distributor must indemnify iMine for claims from its sales activities. Confidential information must be protected, and breach allows immediate termination. Either party may terminate with 90 days' notice or for cause after a 30-day cure period.

termination_clause: This Agreement may be terminated by MusclePharm only: (i) In the event Endorser is convicted of a felony. (ii) In the event Endorser is in material breach or default, after 30-day cure period. (iii) Upon fifteen days prior written notice, if death, physical disability, or other incapacity lasting more than eight weeks causes Endorser to be unable to perform a material amount of the services. This Agreement may be terminated by the AS Parties only: (i) If MusclePharm defaults under any indebtedness over $1,000,000 and such default results in acceleration. (ii) If MusclePharm dissolves, liquidates, becomes insolvent, or commences bankruptcy. (iii) If MusclePharm or its CEO/CFO is charged with a felony, sued by a governmental agency, receives a subpoena, or becomes subject to an investigation that could have a material adverse effect. (iv) If the AS Parties reasonably determine that any MusclePharm products are harmful to the human body or unsafe. (v) If MusclePharm is in material breach or default, after 30-day cure period.
confidentiality_clause: The parties acknowledge that during the course of this Agreement the parties will provide to each other certain proprietary and confidential information that is held and maintained confidentially by each party. Each party shall be entitled to share such confidential information received by such party only with such party’s representatives, legal and accounting advisors who shall agree to be bound by the confidentiality obligations set forth in this Section 26. During the term of this Agreement and for three (3) years thereafter, each party shall hold in strict confidence all such information. This obligation shall not apply to any information which: (a) becomes known to the general public through no fault of either party; (b) is required to be disclosed in the enforcement of rights hereunder, or (c) is required to be disclosed by any state or federal statute, regulation or court order.
liability_clause: Indemnification: (a) The AS Parties will indemnify MusclePharm for breaches of warranty or agreement, but their liability shall not exceed the after-tax value of the Cash Consideration received by Lender. (b) MusclePharm indemnifies the AS Parties for claims arising from product defects, misrepresentations, advertising, or breach of agreement. Additionally, under Section 24, the AS Parties disclaim all implied warranties and 'shall not be liable for special, consequential, or punitive damages.' MusclePharm must also maintain liability insurance of at least $5,000,000 per occurrence naming the AS Parties as additional insureds.
summary: This Endorsement Licensing and Co-Branding Agreement (July 26, 2013) between Arnold Schwarzenegger’s entities (Lender, Endorser, Fitness Publications) and MusclePharm Corporation establishes a co-branded product line (AS Product Line) of dietary supplements. MusclePharm gains worldwide rights to use Schwarzenegger’s name, likeness, and trademarks for promotion and sale, subject to quality controls and approvals. Key obligations: Endorser must make appearances, provide endorsements, and create promotional content; MusclePharm must pay royalties (10% of Net Sales), guaranteed minimum royalties (e.g., $1.5M in year one), and issue 780,000 restricted shares. MusclePharm is responsible for marketing ($3-5M annually), insurance, and compliance with safety standards. Notable risks include termination triggers (felony, breach, insolvency, product safety concerns), indemnification caps on AS Parties’ liability, strict confidentiality for three years post-termination, and a non-competition clause for Endorser lasting one year after termination.

summary: This Joint Venture Agreement between Kiromic Biopharma and Molipharma S.R.L. establishes a collaboration to jointly research and develop clinical trial programs in oncology (specifically ovarian cancer) and a Covid-19 vaccine. Kiromic is primarily responsible for funding the oncology program and sharing its patents and know-how for the vaccine, while Molipharma provides tissue samples, utilizes UCSC as a clinical trial site, and finances the vaccine program. Both parties contribute to a steering committee and technical coordination. Key obligations include mutual confidentiality (Clause 13), joint ownership of intellectual property from results (Clause 6), and specific economic rights allocation based on territory and program. Notable risks include termination rights for insolvency, breach, or failure to meet obligations (Clauses 9 and 10), with penalties such as reimbursement of expenses and additional damages upon default by Kiromic (Clause 9). Indemnification is provided for broker fees (Clause 12), and parties are liable for damage caused by their staff (Clause 5).

summary: This Endorsement Agreement between Healthcare Distribution Specialists LLC (HDS) and Paul Silas (Celebrity) engages Celebrity to endorse HDS's product Clotamin in the U.S. for a one-year term. Key obligations: HDS pays $50,000 (in installments) plus a one-year product supply, and may use Celebrity's name, image, and likeness in a TV commercial (limited to three markets), on its website, and in press releases, subject to Celebrity's prior approval and a required disclaimer that Silas is not a medical authority. Celebrity must provide one production session (up to eight hours) and serve as a board advisor (name-only role). Notable risks: HDS can terminate for uncured breach after 10 days' notice; Celebrity can terminate for HDS insolvency, non-payment (with 3-day cure), or uncured breach. HDS indemnifies Celebrity against product liability and other claims, and must maintain $1M+ insurance coverage naming Celebrity as additional insured. Celebrity is barred from endorsing other multivitamins during the term.

summary: This agreement grants Bank of America a perpetual, irrevocable license to Cardlytics' software, along with customization and maintenance services. Bank of America's key obligations include paying license fees and maintaining confidentiality. Cardlytics must deliver operable software, provide maintenance, and adhere to strict data security requirements. Notable risks include Cardlytics' broad indemnification obligations for IP infringement, negligence, or breach, as well as liability for harmful code. Limitations of liability exclude consequential damages but do not cap indemnity or confidentiality breaches. Bank of America can terminate for convenience on 45 days' notice, and either party may terminate for material breach. The agreement also imposes extensive information security and business continuity obligations on Cardlytics, with potential termination for non-compliance.

summary: This Outsourcing Agreement between Paratek Pharmaceuticals (Customer) and CARBOGEN AMCIS (Supplier) governs the manufacture and supply of a pharmaceutical active ingredient (Product). Supplier agrees to produce Product in accordance with specifications, cGMP, and the Quality Agreement, using Customer-supplied materials where applicable. Customer provides rolling forecasts and binding purchase orders, and pays fees as per Exhibit C. Key obligations include Supplier’s warranty of compliance, Customer’s responsibility for regulatory filings, and confidentiality of shared information. Notable risks include termination rights for material breach or insolvency, strict limitations on liability (excluding consequential damages and capping direct damages), mutual indemnification for third-party claims (product liability, IP infringement), and recall cost allocation based on fault. The agreement also provides for technology transfer to backup suppliers and survival of key provisions after termination.

summary: The Development and Option Agreement between Harpoon Therapeutics and AbbVie Biotechnology grants AbbVie an option to license Harpoon's intellectual property for developing and commercializing HPN217 (a BCMA-targeting compound) and related products worldwide. Harpoon initially conducts development activities under an Initial Development Plan, with AbbVie having the option to exercise a license upon receiving an Opt-In Development Report. If exercised, AbbVie gains exclusive rights to develop, manufacture, and commercialize the licensed products, with Harpoon providing technology transfer and support. Key obligations include Harpoon's performance of initial development and AbbVie's payment of upfront, milestone, and royalty payments. Notable risks include potential termination for material breach or insolvency, confidentiality obligations extending beyond termination, and indemnification provisions covering third-party claims. The agreement also contains exclusivity provisions restricting Harpoon from competing products and allows AbbVie to terminate or reduce royalties under certain conditions.
